commit.message:
Revert patchApplicator.ts to working version (remove computeMinimalDiff)

The computeMinimalDiff function (Uint16Array, Set, Map, Array.from)
was crashing the /api/claude serverless function at module load time.
Even though claude.ts only imports parsePatchBlocks and applyPatches,
the bundler includes all module code — and the crash prevents the
handler from ever running.

commit.message:
feat: implement E2E encrypted DMs — full client-side crypto stack

New files:
- src/main/e2e-keys.ts: Device key management (ECDH P-256 identity,
  signed pre-key, one-time pre-keys). Keys stored encrypted via
  Electron safeStorage (DPAPI).
- src/main/e2e-crypto.ts: X3DH key exchange + Double Ratchet protocol.
  HKDF-SHA256 chain key derivation, AES-256-GCM per-message encryption.
  Forward secrecy via ephemeral key ratcheting. Sessions stored encrypted.
- src/renderer/stores/e2eStore.ts: Renderer-side E2E state. Handles
  key bundle upload, OTP key replenishment, encrypt/decrypt via IPC.

Modified files:
- src/main/ipc.ts: 12 new E2E IPC handlers (init, encrypt, decrypt,
  key bundle, session management)
- src/preload/index.ts: electronAPI.e2e namespace exposed to renderer
- src/renderer/App.tsx: Auto-init E2E + upload keys on authentication
- src/renderer/hooks/useMessages.ts: Message type extended with
  is_encrypted + encrypted_content fields
- src/renderer/components/messages/MessageItem.tsx: Lock icon +
  placeholder for encrypted messages, decrypted content display

Server contract: Consumes PUT /api/e2e/keys, GET /api/e2e/keys/:userId,
POST /api/e2e/keys/one-time (server implementing per SRVREQ).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(cdk): replace nvm with codebuild runtime-versions for node 20

source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh fails with "not found" because codebuild runs
the buildspec under /bin/sh (dash), not /bin/bash. source is a bash
built-in that does not exist in sh.

switch from shellstep + nvm to codebuildstep + partialBuildSpec so that
node 20 is selected via codebuild's native runtime-versions mechanism in
the install phase. this is shell-agnostic and the recommended approach.

also moves the ssm role policy from synthcodebuildefaults into the step's
own rolePolicyStatements, which is the correct place when using codebuildstep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix contract lookup for Core DAO and Waterfall chains

Core DAO's Blockscout API requires an API key (returns 401).
Waterfall has no Blockscout API at all. Both now fall back to
reading name(), symbol(), and supportsInterface() directly from
the contract via free RPC calls.

Lookup flow:
1. Try Blockscout API if available
2. If Blockscout fails or unavailable, read directly from contract
   via the chain's free RPC endpoints
3. Checks ERC-721 interface support

Fix speed curve parsing and mid-speech cutting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Method JIT: Unified diagnostic tool (Diagnose)

One-call diagnostic: Diagnose(proto, args) runs the full pipeline
and compares IR interpreter vs native execution.

diagnose.go (292 lines):
  BuildGraph → Validate → Pipeline(TypeSpec/ConstProp/DCE) with dump →
  RegAlloc → Interpret → Compile → Execute → Compare.
  Reports: IR before/after, pass diffs, validation, regalloc map,
  interpreter result, native result, match/mismatch verdict.

diagnose_test.go (215 lines):
  7 tests: simple add, for-loop with pass diffs, fib with deopt,
  float arithmetic, validation check, report string format, regalloc map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pagination, chunked sync, and Supabase 1000-row limit

Pagination:
- Proper pagination with «, ‹, page numbers, ›, » buttons
- Page numbers with ellipsis for large collections (1 ... 5 6 7 ... 80)
- Current page highlighted in purple
- Same component used top and bottom
- Starts at page 1 (tokens #1-100)

Supabase row limit fix:
- Fetches token IDs in batches of 1000 to bypass Supabase's
  default 1000-row limit. Collections with 8000+ NFTs now
  paginate correctly.

Chunked sync:
- syncContractChunk() processes 500 tokens per API call
- Frontend calls chunks sequentially with progress messages
  ("Syncing... chunk 3 (1500 so far of ~8000)")
- Each chunk fits within Vercel's 60s timeout
- First chunk clears old data, subsequent chunks append
- maxDuration set to 60s in route config

* fix: use localStorage for PKCE state to survive OAuth redirects

sessionStorage is lost when the browser opens a new tab or browsing
context changes during the OAuth redirect to login.divine.video.
Switch to localStorage (as recommended by the SDK docs) so the PKCE
code verifier persists reliably. The SDK cleans up after exchangeCode.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve role emoji rendering, add CSP, split vendor bundles

- Role picker: render custom emoji images instead of raw :shortcode:
  text in both RolePickerModal and RoleReactionEmbed (resolves via
  emojiStore manifest, matching RoleReactionsPanel pattern)

- Security: add strict Content-Security-Policy meta tag to renderer
  HTML — locks down script/style/img/connect sources, whitelists

commit.message:
Fix tool error handling: raise exceptions instead of returning error strings

Tools now raise exceptions (ToolError/RuntimeError) on failure instead of
returning error dict strings, enabling ToolRetryMiddleware to catch and retry.
Also updated agent prompts to retry on failures instead of giving up.

- AnalysisTool: raise ToolError for CSV/plotting tool failures
- GeneralTool: raise ToolError for file/search/HTTP tool failures
- mcp_server: raise RuntimeError for 14 PyBullet tool execution errors
- gazebo_mcp_server: raise RuntimeError for spawn_model/set_model_state
- MainAgentPrompt: retry on error instead of abandoning task
- SimulationAgentPrompt: retry on tool failure instead of returning error

repository.name: robotagent
repository.description: Implement of my bachelor degree paper

commit.message:
FEAT: LLM-generated Manim pipeline + data charts + animation tool

Architectural upgrade: Manim animations are now LLM-generated code rather
than hardcoded 3-branch templates, giving AI agents full creative flexibility.

tools/manim_codegen.py (NEW):
- generate_and_run_manim() — LLM generates complete Manim Python class,
  static-validates it (syntax + deprecated-API checks), runs in sandbox,
  and retries up to 5 times with error injection on failure
- System prompt includes version constraints (ManimCE v0.20.x), deprecated-API
  blocklist, and 3 few-shot examples for reliable code generation
- Falls back gracefully to hardcoded template if all retries fail

agents/latex_agent.py:
- _option_b_node now calls manim_codegen first; falls back to
  latex_transparent.py only if LLM generation exhausts retries
- LatexState gains `prompt` field for custom animation descriptions
- LLM receives a description derived from latex_expression + animation_type
  (not just an enum), enabling richer, semantically correct animations

manim_scripts/latex_transparent.py:
- Fix: strip \\[...\\] and $...$ wrappers before passing to MathTex —
  MathTex adds its own align* environment; double-wrapping caused LaTeX
  compile errors (the root cause of all Manim test failures)

manim_scripts/data_chart_scene.py (NEW):
- DataChartScene supporting: bar_chart, line_chart, pie_chart, counter, scatter
- bar_chart: BarChart + LaggedStart bar labels
- line_chart: Axes + polyline + LaggedStart dots
- pie_chart: AnnularSector slices + legend
- counter: ValueTracker + always_redraw animated number
- scatter: Axes + LaggedStart GrowFromCenter dots

blender_scripts/thumbnail.py:
- Fix: wrap scene.cycles.denoiser = "OPENIMAGEDENOISE" in try/except —
  the apt Blender 3.4.1 build on Render.com has OIDN enum = () so the
  assignment raised TypeError and crashed all thumbnail renders
- Reduce samples 128→32 (OIDN is unavailable, NLM is the fallback)

tools/manim_runner.py:
- Inject args as both LATEX_SCENE_ARGS and CHART_SCENE_ARGS so
  data_chart_scene.py can read them alongside legacy latex scripts

tools/render_tools.py + server.py:
- impl_generate_animation: LLM Manim from natural language + optional
  Blender composite background
- impl_generate_chart: data viz via DataChartScene
- blender_generate_animation MCP tool (9th tool)
- blender_generate_chart MCP tool (10th tool)
- Updated blender_generate_latex signature: adds `prompt` override param
- Both new tools registered in TOOL_HANDLERS job queue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Method JIT: top-4 optimization audit fixes

Fix 1 (P0): Enable optimization passes in tiering path
- tiering.go now runs TypeSpecializePass, ConstPropPass, DCEPass
  before register allocation when auto-promoting from interpreter
- Previously only the benchmark/test harness ran these passes;
  production tiering path skipped them entirely

Fix 2 (P0): Add float op emission for type-specialized ops
- OpAddFloat/OpSubFloat/OpMulFloat: emit direct FP arithmetic
  (FMOV+FADD/FSUB/FMUL+FMOV) instead of falling through to NOP
- OpLtFloat/OpLeFloat: emit FCMP+CSET (not integer CMP, which
  gives wrong ordering for IEEE 754 floats)
- OpNegFloat: emit FMOV+FNEG+FMOV

Fix 3 (P1): Pin NB_TagBool in X25 + TBNZ for branches
- Pin NB_TagBool (0xFFFD000000000000) in X25, saving a LoadImm64
  (2-3 instructions) on every comparison result boxing
- Replace 3-instruction branch sequence (LoadImm64+AND+CBNZ) with
  single TBNZ instruction testing bit 0 of NaN-boxed bool directly
- Add TBZ/TBNZ to assembler with 14-bit offset fixup support

Fix 4 (correctness): Box raw ints in phi moves
- When type-specialized int ops (OpAddInt etc.) produce raw int64
  values in registers, phi moves must box them before transferring
  to the phi destination register (which expects NaN-boxed values)
- Without this fix, the tiering path with optimization passes
  produced wrong results (raw int 55 interpreted as float 2.7e-322)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: middleware now forwards org/user context to API routes

Two bugs: (1) /api/ was in PUBLIC_PREFIXES so all API routes bypassed
middleware entirely — the x-org-id header was never set. (2) Headers
were set on the response instead of the request, so API route handlers
couldn't read them. Fixed by removing /api/ from public prefixes and
using NextResponse.next({ request: { headers } }) to forward context.

commit.message:
Fix mobile nav: restore logo/hamburger, fix iOS scroll bleed

The Checkpoint 3 refactor replaced the branded mobile nav header
(logo + animated hamburger-to-X) with a generic "Menu" + X button.
It also introduced scroll bleed-through on iOS Safari.

Three root causes fixed:
- Portal to document.body broke stacking context (overlay above nav bar)
- backdrop-blur on <nav> creates containing block trapping fixed children
- overflow:hidden alone doesn't prevent iOS Safari background scrolling

Portal now targets [data-sticky-nav-root] (sibling of <nav>, same stacking
context). Scroll lock uses position:fixed on body with scroll restore.
Overlay layout matches pre-refactor structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite LW NFT sync: free RPC primary, Alchemy only for ID listing

New architecture:
1. Alchemy getNFTsForContract (no metadata, lightweight) to get
   token IDs only — or Blockscout as fallback
2. Alchemy getOwnersForContract to identify burned tokens
3. For each live token: call tokenURI() on the contract via FREE
   RPC (polygon.drpc.org, cloudflare-eth.com, etc.)
4. Fetch metadata directly from the tokenURI response — handles:
   - HTTP URLs (nftdata.lightningworks.io)
   - IPFS URIs (converted to ipfs.io gateway)
   - On-chain base64 data URIs (decoded inline)
5. Store live metadata with correct revealed images

Benefits:
- Always gets current metadata (not Alchemy's stale cache)
- Revealed NFTs show their unique cover art
- Works for ALL LW contracts regardless of metadata storage
- Alchemy only used for lightweight enumeration (minimal quota)
- 20 concurrent metadata fetches per batch for speed
- Handles up to 10,000 NFTs per contract
- Burned tokens filtered out

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fetch live metadata from tokenURI, increase sync limit to 10k

Fixes two issues:
1. Alchemy caches stale NFT metadata, showing unrevealed cover art
   even after tokens have been revealed. Now fetches live metadata
   directly from each token's tokenURI (e.g. nftdata.lightningworks.io)
   to get the current revealed image and attributes.
2. Page limit increased from 50 to 100 (max 10,000 NFTs per contract)
   to handle large collections like Siege Worlds (~8,000 live tokens).

Falls back to Alchemy's cached metadata if tokenURI fetch fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
security: protect all credentials with OS-level encryption (DPAPI)

CRITICAL FIX — both stores now encrypted before alpha release:

1. Settings store (savedServers with tokens) was plaintext JSON.
   Now encrypted with electron-store encryptionKey.

2. Encryption key was plaintext in keychain.json. Now stored as
   an opaque binary blob via Electron safeStorage (DPAPI on
   Windows, Keychain on macOS, libsecret on Linux). Only the
   current OS user session can decrypt it.

3. Migration: automatically reads old plaintext settings.json and
   keychain.json, imports data into encrypted stores, and deletes
   the old plaintext files.

On-disk files after this change:
- encryption-key.bin (OS-encrypted, unreadable by other programs)
- settings.json (AES-encrypted with OS-protected key)
- auth.json (AES-encrypted with OS-protected key)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix /api/claude 500: dynamic import patchApplicator to prevent module crash

Every POST to /api/claude has returned 500 since commit 292b366 added
a static import of computeMinimalDiff from lib/patchApplicator.ts. The
serverless function fails at module initialization (handler never runs,
no catch blocks fire, no error logs). The patchApplicator module likely
produces broken bundle output with the ES5 tsconfig target.

Fix: convert static import to dynamic import() inside the handler,
loaded only when file operations need it. Add module-level diagnostic
console.log to verify function initialization in Vercel runtime logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: ampliar busqueda OB y eliminar filtro de toques redundante

- buscar_ob: busca en 45 velas 4H (antes 25) → captura OBs hasta ~7 dias atras
- analizar: 200 velas 4H descargadas (antes 100) para cubrir el historial ampliado
- _trade_tendencia: elimina filtro contar_toques (redundante con en_ob + confirmacion)
